citations.narrative:
There was no Record of Acquisition and Dogs and Cats on Hand (APHIS Form 7005) with the required information
available at the time of inspection for the cats. The facility does have a system to document the inventory information for
their cats; however, they are not currently using the required APHIS Form 7005. The acquisition form (APHIS 7005) is
necessary in order to accurately track animals being used in regulated activities and to ensure all required acquisition
information is documented and available to the APHIS inspector. Each dealer or exhibitor shall use the Record of
Acquisition and Dogs and Cats on Hand (APHIS 7005) and Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats (APHIS 7006) to
make, keep, and maintain the required information.
Correct by 03/13/2026.

The fine wire, metal meshing along the bottoms of 8 doors within the sheltered cat enclosure was stained dark brown and
had excessively rusted, corroded surfaces. Additionally, the bottom portion of a metal storage cabinet inside the enclosure
was stained dark brown with corrosion and flaking rust. Surfaces that come in contact with the cats which contain
excessive rust cannot be adequately cleaned and sanitized and may increase the risk of disease hazards. Any surfaces
that come in contact with the cats must be free of excessive rust that prevents the required cleaning and sanitization.
Correct by 03/13/2026.

In a food storage room, there were three open bags of hay and an open bag of grain feed used for the deer. Open
supplies of food may attract vermin or other pests and become contaminated, increasing the potential risk of disease or
other health hazards. Supplies of food and bedding must be stored in facilities which adequately protect such supplies
against deterioration, molding, or contamination by vermin. A system of adequately protected food storage must be in
place.
Correct 03/13/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.75(a)(2) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
There was no Record of Acquisition and Dogs and Cats on Hand (APHIS Form 7005) with the required information 
available at the time of inspection for the cats. The facility does have a system to document the inventory information for 
their cats; however, they are not currently using the required APHIS Form 7005. The acquisition form (APHIS 7005) is 
necessary in order to accurately track animals being used in regulated activities and to ensure all required acquisition 
information is documented and available to the APHIS inspector. Each dealer or exhibitor shall use the Record of 
Acquisition and Dogs and Cats on Hand (APHIS 7005) and Record of Disposition of Dogs and Cats (APHIS 7006) to 
make, keep, and maintain the required information. 
 
Correct by 03/13/2026. 
3.1(c)(1)(i) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
The fine wire, metal meshing along the bottoms of 8 doors within the sheltered cat enclosure was stained dark brown and 
had excessively rusted, corroded surfaces. Additionally, the bottom portion of a metal storage cabinet inside the enclosure 
was stained dark brown with corrosion and flaking rust. Surfaces that come in contact with the cats which contain 
excessive rust cannot be adequately cleaned and sanitized and may increase the risk of disease hazards. Any surfaces 
that come in contact with the cats must be free of excessive rust that prevents the required cleaning and sanitization. 
 
Correct by 03/13/2026. 
3.125(c) 
   
Facilities, general. 
In a food storage room, there were three open bags of hay and an open bag of grain feed used for the deer. Open 
supplies of food may attract vermin or other pests and become contaminated, increasing the potential risk of disease or 
other health hazards. Supplies of food and bedding must be stored in facilities which adequately protect such supplies 
against deterioration, molding, or contamination by vermin. A system of adequately protected food storage must be in 
place.

Correct 03/13/2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
